1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: This is the revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave him to show his servants what must happen soon. Jesus made it known by sending his angel to his servant John.
John told everything he saw — the word of God and the message of Jesus Christ.
God will bless the one who reads the words of this prophecy out loud, and he will bless those who hear it and obey what is written, because the time is near.
From John, to the seven churches in the province of Asia: Grace and peace to you from the One who is, and who was, and who is coming, and from the seven Spirits in front of his throne,
and from Jesus Christ, the faithful witness, the first to rise from the dead, and the ruler of the kings of the earth. Jesus loves us and has set us free from our sins by his blood.
He has made us a kingdom and priests to serve his God and Father. To him be glory and power forever and ever. Amen.
Look, he is coming with the clouds! Every eye will see him, even those who pierced him. All the peoples of the earth will cry because of him. Yes, this is true. Amen.
I am the Alpha and the Omega — the beginning and the end, says the Lord God, the One who is, and who was, and who is coming, the All-Powerful.

I, John, am your brother. I share with you in the suffering, the kingdom, and the patient endurance that are ours in Jesus. I was on the island of Patmos because of the word of God and the message of Jesus.
On the Lord's day the Spirit took hold of me, and I heard behind me a loud voice like a trumpet.
It said: Write in a book what you see, and send it to the seven churches: Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.
I turned around to see who was speaking to me. When I turned, I saw seven gold lampstands,
and among the lampstands was someone who looked like the Son of Man. He was wearing a long robe with a gold band around his chest.
His head and hair were white as wool, white as snow, and his eyes were like flames of fire.
His feet were like bronze glowing in a furnace, and his voice was like the sound of rushing waters.
He held seven stars in his right hand, and a sharp double-edged sword came out of his mouth. His face was like the sun shining at its brightest.
When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead man. But he placed his right hand on me and said: Do not be afraid. I am the First and the Last.
I am the Living One. I was dead, but now look — I am alive forever and ever! And I hold the keys of death and of the place of the dead.
So write down what you have seen, what is happening now, and what will happen later.
Here is the meaning of the seven stars you saw in my right hand and the seven gold lampstands: The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the seven lampstands are the seven churches.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Write this to the angel of the church in Ephesus: These are the words of the One who holds the seven stars in his right hand and walks among the seven gold lampstands.

I know what you do. I know your hard work and your patience. I know you cannot put up with evil people. You have tested those who claim to be apostles but are not, and you have found them to be liars.
You have kept going and have suffered for my name, and you have not given up.
But I have this against you: you have left the love you had at first.
Remember how far you have fallen! Turn back and do the things you did at first. If you do not turn back, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place.
But you do have this in your favor: you hate the practices of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.
Anyone who has ears should listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. To the one who wins the victory, I will give the right to eat from the tree of life, which is in the paradise of God.
Write this to the angel of the church in Smyrna: These are the words of the First and the Last, the One who died and came back to life.
I know your suffering and your poverty — but you are rich! I know the evil things said about you by those who claim to be Jews but are not. They are really a group that belongs to Satan.

Do not be afraid of what you are about to suffer. The devil will throw some of you into prison to test you, and you will suffer for ten days. Be faithful, even to the point of death, and I will give you the crown of life.
Anyone who has ears should listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. The one who wins the victory will not be hurt at all by the second death.
Write this to the angel of the church in Pergamum: These are the words of the One who has the sharp double-edged sword.

I know where you live — where Satan has his throne. Yet you hold firmly to my name. You did not give up your faith in me, even in the days when Antipas, my faithful witness, was put to death in your city, where Satan lives.

But I have a few things against you. Some of your people hold to the teaching of Balaam, who taught Balak to lead the people of Israel into sin, so that they ate food offered to idols and committed sexual sin.
You also have some who hold to the teaching of the Nicolaitans.
So turn back from your sin! If you do not, I will come to you soon and fight against them with the sword of my mouth.

Anyone who has ears should listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. To the one who wins the victory, I will give some of the hidden manna. I will also give him a white stone with a new name written on it, a name that no one knows except the one who receives it.
Write this to the angel of the church in Thyatira: These are the words of the Son of God, whose eyes are like flames of fire and whose feet are like polished bronze.
I know what you do — your love, your faith, your service, and your patience. I know that you are doing more now than you did at first.
But I have this against you: you allow that woman Jezebel, who calls herself a prophet, to lead my servants into sin. She teaches them to commit sexual sin and to eat food offered to idols.
I gave her time to turn away from her sin, but she does not want to.
So I will throw her on a bed of suffering. And unless they turn away from her ways, I will make those who commit adultery with her suffer terribly.
I will strike her children dead. Then all the churches will know that I am the One who searches hearts and minds, and I will reward each of you according to what you have done.
Now to the rest of you in Thyatira, who do not follow her teaching and have not learned what some call the deep secrets of Satan: I will not put any other burden on you.
Just hold on to what you have until I come.
To the one who wins the victory and keeps doing what I want until the end, I will give authority over the nations.
He will rule them with an iron rod and break them to pieces like clay pots — just as I myself received authority from my Father.
And I will also give him the morning star.
Anyone who has ears should listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Write this to the angel of the church in Sardis: These are the words of the One who has the seven Spirits of God and the seven stars. I know what you do. People say you are alive, but you are dead.
Wake up! Strengthen what is left before it dies completely, because I have found that your works are not finished in the sight of my God.
So remember what you received and heard. Obey it, and turn back from your sin. If you do not wake up, I will come like a thief, and you will not know at what time I will come to you.
But you have a few people in Sardis who have kept their clothes clean. They will walk with me, dressed in white, because they are worthy.
The one who wins the victory will be dressed in white. I will never erase his name from the book of life, but I will say that he is mine in front of my Father and his angels.
Anyone who has ears should listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.

Write this to the angel of the church in Philadelphia: These are the words of the One who is holy and true, who holds the key of David. What he opens, no one can shut, and what he shuts, no one can open.
I know what you do. Look, I have placed in front of you an open door that no one can shut. I know that you have only a little strength, yet you have obeyed my word and have not denied my name.
Look, I will make those who belong to Satan's group — those who claim to be Jews but are not, who are lying — come and bow down at your feet. Then they will know that I have loved you.
Because you have obeyed my command to be patient, I will keep you safe from the time of trouble that is coming on the whole world to test those who live on the earth.
I am coming soon. Hold on to what you have, so that no one takes your crown.

The one who wins the victory I will make a pillar in the temple of my God, and he will never have to leave it. I will write on him the name of my God and the name of the city of my God — the new Jerusalem, which comes down out of heaven from my God. And I will also write on him my new name.
Anyone who has ears should listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
Write this to the angel of the church in Laodicea: These are the words of the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the ruler of God's creation.
I know what you do. You are neither cold nor hot. I wish you were one or the other!
But because you are lukewarm — neither hot nor cold — I am about to spit you out of my mouth.
You say: I am rich. I have everything I need. But you do not realize that you are wretched, pitiful, poor, blind, and naked.

So I advise you to buy from me gold made pure by fire, so you can be truly rich. Buy white clothes to wear, so you can cover the shame of your nakedness. And buy medicine to put on your eyes, so you can see.
I correct and discipline everyone I love. So be serious, and turn back from your sin.
Look, I stand at the door and knock. If anyone hears my voice and opens the door, I will come in and eat with him, and he will eat with me.
To the one who wins the victory I will give the right to sit with me on my throne, just as I won the victory and sat down with my Father on his throne.
Anyone who has ears should listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.

4.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: After this I looked, and there in front of me was a door standing open in heaven. The voice I had first heard speaking to me like a trumpet said: Come up here, and I will show you what must happen after this.
At once the Spirit took hold of me, and there before me was a throne in heaven, with someone sitting on it.
The one sitting there shone like precious stones — like jasper and carnelian. A rainbow that looked like an emerald circled the throne.
Around the throne were twenty-four other thrones, and on them sat twenty-four elders. They were dressed in white and had gold crowns on their heads.
From the throne came flashes of lightning, rumblings, and thunder. In front of the throne, seven torches were blazing. These are the seven Spirits of God.

Also in front of the throne there was what looked like a sea of glass, clear as crystal. In the center, around the throne, were four living creatures, and they were covered with eyes, in front and in back.
The first living creature was like a lion, the second was like an ox, the third had a face like a man, and the fourth was like a flying eagle.

Each of the four living creatures had six wings and was covered with eyes all around, even under its wings. Day and night they never stop saying: Holy, holy, holy is the Lord God Almighty, who was, and is, and is coming.
Whenever the living creatures give glory, honor, and thanks to the One who sits on the throne and who lives forever and ever,
the twenty-four elders fall down before him who sits on the throne and worship him who lives forever and ever. They lay their crowns in front of the throne and say:
You are worthy, our Lord and God, to receive glory, honor, and power, because you made all things. By your will they were created and have their being.

5.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Then I saw in the right hand of the One who sat on the throne a scroll with writing on both sides. It was sealed with seven seals.
And I saw a mighty angel calling out in a loud voice: Who is worthy to break the seals and open the scroll?
But no one in heaven or on earth or under the earth could open the scroll or even look inside it.
I cried and cried, because no one was found who was worthy to open the scroll or look inside.
Then one of the elders said to me: Do not cry. Look! The Lion of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has won the victory. He is able to open the scroll and its seven seals.

Then I saw a Lamb standing in the center, near the throne and among the four living creatures and the elders. The Lamb looked as if it had been killed. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven Spirits of God sent out into all the earth.
The Lamb went and took the scroll from the right hand of the One who sat on the throne.

When he took it, the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb. Each one had a harp, and they held golden bowls full of incense, which are the prayers of God's people.

And they sang a new song: You are worthy to take the scroll and to open its seals, because you were killed, and with your blood you bought people for God from every tribe and language and people and nation.
You have made them a kingdom and priests to serve our God, and they will rule on the earth.
Then I looked, and I heard the voices of many angels around the throne, the living creatures, and the elders. There were thousands upon thousands, and ten thousand times ten thousand.
In a loud voice they sang: Worthy is the Lamb who was killed, to receive power, riches, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and praise!

Then I heard every creature in heaven, on earth, under the earth, and in the sea — all that is in them — singing: To the One who sits on the throne and to the Lamb be praise, honor, glory, and power forever and ever!
The four living creatures said: Amen. And the elders fell down and worshiped.

6.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Then I watched as the Lamb broke open the first of the seven seals. I heard one of the four living creatures say in a voice like thunder: Come!
I looked, and there before me was a white horse. Its rider held a bow, and he was given a crown. He rode out as a winner, to win the victory.
When the Lamb broke open the second seal, I heard the second living creature say: Come!
Then another horse came out, a fiery red one. Its rider was given power to take peace from the earth and to make people kill each other. He was given a large sword.
When the Lamb broke open the third seal, I heard the third living creature say: Come! I looked, and there before me was a black horse. Its rider held a pair of scales in his hand.

Then I heard what sounded like a voice among the four living creatures, saying: A day's pay for a quart of wheat, and a day's pay for three quarts of barley. But do not harm the olive oil and the wine!
When the Lamb broke open the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say: Come!

I looked, and there before me was a pale horse. Its rider was named Death, and the place of the dead followed close behind him. They were given power over a fourth of the earth, to kill by sword, by hunger, by disease, and by the wild animals of the earth.
When the Lamb broke open the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of those who had been killed because of the word of God and the testimony they had held.
They called out in a loud voice: How long, holy and true Lord, until you judge the people of the earth and pay them back for our blood?
Then each of them was given a white robe, and they were told to wait a little longer, until the full number of their fellow servants and brothers and sisters were killed, just as they had been.
I watched as the Lamb broke open the sixth seal. There was a great earthquake. The sun turned black like rough black cloth, and the whole moon turned red like blood.
The stars in the sky fell to the earth, like figs dropping from a tree when it is shaken by a strong wind.
The sky was rolled up like a scroll, and every mountain and island was moved from its place.
Then the kings of the earth, the important men, the army commanders, the rich, the powerful, and everyone else — slave and free — hid in caves and among the rocks of the mountains.
They called to the mountains and the rocks: Fall on us and hide us from the face of the One who sits on the throne and from the anger of the Lamb!
For the great day of their anger has come, and who can stand?

7.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: After this I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth. They were holding back the four winds of the earth, so that no wind would blow on the land or the sea or on any tree.
Then I saw another angel coming up from the east, holding the seal of the living God. He called out in a loud voice to the four angels who had been given power to harm the land and the sea:
Do not harm the land or the sea or the trees until we have put a seal on the foreheads of the servants of our God.
Then I heard the number of those who were sealed: 144,000 from all the tribes of Israel.
From the tribe of Judah 12,000 were sealed, from the tribe of Reuben 12,000, from the tribe of Gad 12,000,
from the tribe of Asher 12,000, from the tribe of Naphtali 12,000, from the tribe of Manasseh 12,000,
from the tribe of Simeon 12,000, from the tribe of Levi 12,000, from the tribe of Issachar 12,000,
from the tribe of Zebulun 12,000, from the tribe of Joseph 12,000, and from the tribe of Benjamin 12,000.

After this I looked, and there was a huge crowd that no one could count. They were from every nation, tribe, people, and language, and they stood in front of the throne and in front of the Lamb. They were dressed in white robes and held palm branches in their hands.
They cried out in a loud voice: Salvation belongs to our God, who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb!
All the angels stood around the throne, the elders, and the four living creatures. They fell down on their faces in front of the throne and worshiped God.
They said: Amen! Praise, glory, wisdom, thanks, honor, power, and strength belong to our God forever and ever. Amen!
Then one of the elders asked me: Who are these people dressed in white robes, and where did they come from?
I answered: Sir, you know. And he said: These are the ones who have come out of the great suffering. They have washed their robes and made them white in the blood of the Lamb.
That is why they are in front of the throne of God and serve him day and night in his temple. And the One who sits on the throne will shelter them with his presence.
They will never be hungry or thirsty again. The sun will not beat down on them, nor will any burning heat.
For the Lamb in the center of the throne will be their shepherd. He will lead them to springs of living water, and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.

8.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: When the Lamb broke open the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
Then I saw the seven angels who stand before God, and they were given seven trumpets.

Another angel came and stood at the altar, holding a golden bowl for burning incense. He was given a large amount of incense to offer, along with the prayers of all God's people, on the golden altar in front of the throne.
The smoke of the incense, together with the prayers of God's people, went up before God from the angel's hand.
Then the angel took the bowl, filled it with fire from the altar, and threw it down on the earth. There were rumblings, thunder, flashes of lightning, and an earthquake.
Then the seven angels with the seven trumpets got ready to blow them.

The first angel blew his trumpet. Hail and fire mixed with blood were thrown down on the earth. A third of the earth was burned up, a third of the trees were burned up, and all the green grass was burned up.
The second angel blew his trumpet. Something like a huge mountain, all on fire, was thrown into the sea. A third of the sea turned into blood,
a third of the living creatures in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
The third angel blew his trumpet. A great star, burning like a torch, fell from the sky. It fell on a third of the rivers and on the springs of water.
The name of the star is Bitterness. A third of the water turned bitter, and many people died from the water, because it had become bitter.

The fourth angel blew his trumpet. A third of the sun was struck, a third of the moon, and a third of the stars, so that they turned dark. A third of the day had no light, and a third of the night also had no light.

Then I looked, and I heard an eagle flying high in the sky calling out in a loud voice: Terrible, terrible, terrible it will be for those who live on the earth, because of the trumpet blasts the other three angels are about to sound!

9.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: The fifth angel blew his trumpet. I saw a star that had fallen from the sky to the earth, and it was given the key to the deep pit that has no bottom.
It opened the deep pit, and smoke rose from it like the smoke from a huge furnace. The sun and the sky were darkened by the smoke from the pit.
Out of the smoke, locusts came down on the earth, and they were given power like the power of scorpions.
They were told not to harm the grass of the earth or any plant or tree, but only the people who did not have the seal of God on their foreheads.
The locusts were not allowed to kill these people, but only to torture them for five months. The pain they caused was like the sting of a scorpion.
In those days people will look for a way to die, but they will not find it. They will want to die, but death will run away from them.
The locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads they wore what looked like crowns of gold, and their faces looked like human faces.
Their hair was like women's hair, and their teeth were like lions' teeth.
They wore what looked like iron armor on their chests, and the sound of their wings was like the noise of many horses and chariots rushing into battle.
They had tails with stingers, like scorpions, and in their tails was the power to hurt people for five months.
Their king was the angel of the deep pit. His name in Hebrew is Abaddon, and in Greek it is Apollyon (which means Destroyer).
The first terror is past. Two more are still to come.
The sixth angel blew his trumpet. I heard a voice coming from the four corners of the golden altar that stands before God.
It said to the sixth angel who had the trumpet: Set free the four angels who are tied up at the great Euphrates River.
So the four angels were set free. They had been kept ready for this very hour and day and month and year, to kill a third of all people.
The number of the soldiers on horseback was two hundred million. I heard their number.

In my vision the horses and their riders looked like this: their armor was fiery red, dark blue, and yellow like sulfur. The heads of the horses were like lions' heads, and out of their mouths came fire, smoke, and sulfur.
A third of all people were killed by these three things: the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur that came out of the horses' mouths.
The power of the horses was in their mouths and in their tails. Their tails were like snakes, with heads that could bite and hurt people.

The rest of the people, who were not killed by these terrible things, still did not turn away from what they had made with their own hands. They kept on worshiping demons and idols of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood — idols that cannot see or hear or walk.
And they did not turn away from their murders, their witchcraft, their sexual sin, or their stealing.

10.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Then I saw another mighty angel coming down from heaven. He was wrapped in a cloud, with a rainbow over his head. His face was like the sun, and his legs were like pillars of fire.
He held a little scroll, which was open in his hand. He put his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land,
and he shouted in a loud voice, like the roar of a lion. When he shouted, the voices of seven thunders spoke.
When the seven thunders spoke, I was about to write it down, but I heard a voice from heaven say: Keep secret what the seven thunders said. Do not write it down.
Then the angel I had seen standing on the sea and the land raised his right hand to heaven.

He made a promise by the One who lives forever and ever, who made heaven and everything in it, the earth and everything in it, and the sea and everything in it. The angel said: There will be no more delay!
But in the days when the seventh angel is about to blow his trumpet, God's hidden plan will be finished, just as he announced to his servants the prophets.
Then the voice I had heard from heaven spoke to me again: Go and take the open scroll from the hand of the angel who is standing on the sea and the land.
So I went to the angel and asked him to give me the little scroll. He said to me: Take it and eat it. It will turn your stomach sour, but in your mouth it will be sweet as honey.
I took the little scroll from the angel's hand and ate it. In my mouth it was sweet as honey, but after I ate it, my stomach turned sour.
Then I was told: You must prophesy again about many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.

11.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: I was given a measuring stick like a rod and was told: Go and measure the temple of God and the altar, and count the people worshiping there.
But do not measure the outer courtyard. Leave it out, because it has been given to the nations. They will walk all over the holy city for forty-two months.
And I will give power to my two witnesses, and they will prophesy for 1,260 days, dressed in rough cloth that shows sorrow.
These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand before the Lord of the earth.
If anyone tries to harm them, fire comes out of their mouths and destroys their enemies. This is how anyone who wants to harm them must die.

These witnesses have power to shut up the sky so that no rain falls during the time they are prophesying. They also have power to turn the waters into blood and to strike the earth with every kind of plague as often as they want.
When they have finished telling their message, the beast that comes up from the deep pit will make war against them. It will defeat them and kill them.
Their bodies will lie in the street of the great city. The city is spiritually called Sodom and Egypt — the same place where their Lord was nailed to a cross.
For three and a half days, people from every people, tribe, language, and nation will look at their dead bodies and refuse to let them be buried.
The people of the earth will be glad about it and will celebrate. They will send each other gifts, because these two prophets had tortured those who live on the earth.
But after the three and a half days, the breath of life from God entered them, and they stood up on their feet. Everyone who saw them was terrified.
Then the two witnesses heard a loud voice from heaven say to them: Come up here. And they went up to heaven in a cloud, while their enemies watched.

At that very hour there was a great earthquake, and a tenth of the city fell down. Seven thousand people were killed in the earthquake, and the rest were terrified and gave glory to the God of heaven.
The second terror is past. The third terror is coming soon.
The seventh angel blew his trumpet, and there were loud voices in heaven, saying: The kingdom of the world has now become the kingdom of our Lord and of his Christ, and he will rule forever and ever.
The twenty-four elders, who were sitting on their thrones before God, fell on their faces and worshiped God.
They said: We give you thanks, Lord God Almighty, the One who is and who was, because you have taken your great power and have begun to rule.

The nations were angry, but now your anger has come. The time has come to judge the dead and to reward your servants the prophets, your holy people, and all who honor your name, both small and great — and to destroy those who destroy the earth.
Then God's temple in heaven was opened, and inside it the ark of his covenant could be seen. There were flashes of lightning, rumblings, thunder, an earthquake, and a great hailstorm.

12.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: A great sign appeared in heaven: a woman dressed with the sun, with the moon under her feet and a crown of twelve stars on her head.
She was pregnant and cried out in pain, ready to give birth.
Then another sign appeared in heaven: a huge red dragon with seven heads and ten horns, and seven crowns on its heads.

Its tail swept a third of the stars out of the sky and threw them down to the earth. The dragon stood in front of the woman who was about to give birth, so that it could eat her child the moment it was born.
The woman gave birth to a son, a boy who will rule all the nations with an iron rod. But her child was snatched up to God and to his throne.
The woman ran away into the desert, to a place God had prepared for her, where she would be taken care of for 1,260 days.
Then there was war in heaven. Michael and his angels fought against the dragon. The dragon and his angels fought back,
but they were not strong enough, and they lost their place in heaven.
The great dragon was thrown down — that old snake called the devil, or Satan, who leads the whole world astray. He was thrown down to the earth, and his angels were thrown down with him.

Then I heard a loud voice in heaven say: Now the salvation, the power, and the kingdom of our God have come, and the authority of his Christ. For the accuser of our brothers and sisters has been thrown down — the one who accuses them before our God day and night.
They won the victory over him by the blood of the Lamb and by the message they told. They did not love their lives so much that they were afraid to die.
So be glad, you heavens and all who live in them! But how terrible it will be for the earth and the sea, because the devil has come down to you. He is very angry, because he knows his time is short.
When the dragon saw that he had been thrown down to the earth, he chased the woman who had given birth to the boy.

But the woman was given the two wings of a great eagle, so that she could fly to the place prepared for her in the desert. There she would be taken care of for a time, times, and half a time, safe from the snake.
Then the snake poured water out of his mouth like a river, trying to sweep the woman away in the flood.
But the earth helped the woman. The earth opened its mouth and swallowed the river that the dragon had poured out.
The dragon was furious with the woman, and he went off to make war against the rest of her children — those who obey God's commands and hold on to the truth about Jesus.

13.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Then I stood on the sand of the seashore. I saw a beast coming up out of the sea. It had seven heads and ten horns, with ten crowns on its horns. On each head was a name that insulted God.
The beast I saw looked like a leopard, but it had feet like a bear and a mouth like a lion. The dragon gave the beast his power, his throne, and great authority.
One of the beast's heads looked as if it had been wounded so badly it should have died, but the deadly wound had been healed. The whole world was amazed and followed the beast.
People worshiped the dragon because he had given his authority to the beast. They also worshiped the beast and said: Who is like the beast? Who can fight against it?
The beast was allowed to brag and to insult God, and it was given authority to do this for forty-two months.
It opened its mouth to insult God — to insult his name, his dwelling place, and all who live in heaven.
The beast was allowed to make war against God's holy people and to defeat them. It was given authority over every tribe, people, language, and nation.
All who live on the earth will worship the beast — everyone whose name has not been written in the Lamb's book of life since the creation of the world. The Lamb is the one who was killed.
Anyone who has ears should listen.

If anyone is meant to be taken prisoner, he will be taken prisoner. If anyone is meant to be killed with the sword, he will be killed with the sword. This calls for patient endurance and faith from God's holy people.
Then I saw another beast coming up out of the earth. It had two horns like a lamb, but it spoke like a dragon.
It used all the authority of the first beast, acting for it. It made the earth and all who live on it worship the first beast, the one whose deadly wound had been healed.
This second beast did great miracles. It even made fire come down from heaven to the earth while people watched.
Because of the miracles it was allowed to do for the first beast, it fooled the people of the earth. It told them to make an image to honor the beast that had been wounded by the sword and yet lived.
The second beast was allowed to give breath to the image of the first beast, so that the image could speak and could order everyone who refused to worship it to be killed.
It also forced everyone — small and great, rich and poor, free and slave — to receive a mark on the right hand or on the forehead,
so that no one could buy or sell unless he had the mark, which is the name of the beast or the number of its name.
This calls for wisdom. Let the one who has understanding figure out the number of the beast, because it is the number of a man. His number is 666.

14.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Then I looked, and there was the Lamb, standing on Mount Zion. With him were 144,000 people who had his name and his Father's name written on their foreheads.
I heard a sound from heaven like the roar of rushing waters and a loud clap of thunder. The sound I heard was like harpists playing their harps.
They were singing a new song in front of the throne, the four living creatures, and the elders. No one could learn the song except the 144,000 who had been bought from the earth.
These are the ones who kept themselves pure. They follow the Lamb wherever he goes. They were bought from among the people as the first offering to God and to the Lamb.
No lie was found in their mouths; they are without fault.
Then I saw another angel flying high in the sky. He had the eternal good news to announce to those who live on the earth — to every nation, tribe, language, and people.
He said in a loud voice: Honor God and give him glory, because the time has come for him to judge. Worship the One who made the heavens, the earth, the sea, and the springs of water.
A second angel followed and said: Fallen! Fallen is Babylon the Great, which made all the nations drink the wine of her wild and sinful ways.
A third angel followed them and said in a loud voice: If anyone worships the beast and its image and receives its mark on his forehead or on his hand,
he too will drink the wine of God's anger, poured out full strength into the cup of his judgment. He will be tortured with burning sulfur in front of the holy angels and the Lamb.
The smoke of their torture rises forever and ever. There is no rest day or night for those who worship the beast and its image, or for anyone who receives the mark of its name.
This calls for patient endurance from God's holy people, who obey his commands and stay faithful to Jesus.

Then I heard a voice from heaven say: Write this: From now on, blessed are the dead who die belonging to the Lord. Yes, says the Spirit, they will rest from their hard work, for what they have done goes with them.
Then I looked, and there was a white cloud, and sitting on the cloud was someone who looked like the Son of Man. He had a gold crown on his head and a sharp sickle in his hand.
Another angel came out of the temple and called in a loud voice to the one sitting on the cloud: Take your sickle and reap, because the time has come to harvest. The crop of the earth is ripe.
So the one sitting on the cloud swung his sickle over the earth, and the earth was harvested.
Another angel came out of the temple in heaven, and he also had a sharp sickle.

Still another angel, who had charge of the fire, came from the altar. He called in a loud voice to the angel with the sharp sickle: Take your sharp sickle and gather the bunches of grapes from the earth's vine, because its grapes are ripe.
The angel swung his sickle over the earth, gathered the grapes, and threw them into the great winepress of God's anger.
The grapes were crushed in the winepress outside the city, and blood flowed out of the winepress as high as a horse's bridle for a distance of about 180 miles.

15.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Then I saw another sign in heaven, great and amazing: seven angels with the seven last plagues. With these the anger of God is finished.

I saw what looked like a sea of glass mixed with fire. Standing beside the sea were those who had won the victory over the beast, its image, and the number of its name. They held harps given to them by God.
They sang the song of Moses, the servant of God, and the song of the Lamb: Great and amazing are your works, Lord God Almighty. Right and true are your ways, King of the nations.
Who will not honor you, Lord, and bring glory to your name? For you alone are holy. All nations will come and worship you, because your right judgments have been made known.
After this I looked, and the temple in heaven — the tent of the covenant law — was opened.
Out of the temple came the seven angels with the seven plagues. They were dressed in clean, shining linen and wore gold bands around their chests.
Then one of the four living creatures gave to the seven angels seven gold bowls filled with the anger of God, who lives forever and ever.
The temple was filled with smoke from the glory and power of God, and no one could enter the temple until the seven plagues of the seven angels were finished.

16.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Then I heard a loud voice from the temple say to the seven angels: Go and pour out on the earth the seven bowls of God's anger.
The first angel went and poured out his bowl on the earth. Ugly, painful sores broke out on the people who had the mark of the beast and who worshiped its image.
The second angel poured out his bowl on the sea, and it turned into blood like that of a dead person. Every living thing in the sea died.
The third angel poured out his bowl on the rivers and the springs of water, and they turned into blood.
Then I heard the angel in charge of the waters say: You are right, Lord — the One who is and who was, the Holy One — because you have judged this way.
They spilled the blood of your holy people and your prophets, and you have given them blood to drink. They deserve it.
And I heard the altar respond: Yes, Lord God Almighty, your judgments are true and right.
The fourth angel poured out his bowl on the sun, and the sun was allowed to scorch people with fire.
They were burned by the great heat, and they cursed the name of God, who had power over these plagues. But they refused to turn from their sins and give him glory.
The fifth angel poured out his bowl on the throne of the beast, and its kingdom was plunged into darkness. People bit their tongues in pain
and cursed the God of heaven because of their pain and their sores. But they still refused to turn away from what they had done.
The sixth angel poured out his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and its water dried up to prepare the way for the kings from the east.
Then I saw three evil spirits that looked like frogs. They came out of the mouth of the dragon, the mouth of the beast, and the mouth of the false prophet.
They are demonic spirits that do miracles. They go out to the kings of the whole world to gather them for the battle on the great day of God Almighty.
Look, I am coming like a thief! Blessed is the one who stays awake and keeps his clothes with him, so that he will not go naked and be shamefully exposed.
Then the spirits gathered the kings together to the place that in Hebrew is called Armageddon.
The seventh angel poured out his bowl into the air, and a loud voice came from the throne in the temple, saying: It is done!
Then there were flashes of lightning, rumblings, thunder, and a great earthquake. There has never been such a great earthquake since people have been on the earth — it was that powerful.
The great city split into three parts, and the cities of the nations collapsed. God remembered Babylon the Great and made her drink the wine from the cup of his furious anger.
Every island ran away, and the mountains could not be found.
Huge hailstones, each weighing about a hundred pounds, fell from the sky on the people. And they cursed God because of the terrible plague of hail.

17.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: One of the seven angels who had the seven bowls came and said to me: Come, I will show you how the great prostitute will be judged — the one who sits on many waters.
The kings of the earth committed sexual sin with her, and the people of the earth got drunk on the wine of her sinful ways.
Then the angel carried me away in the Spirit into a desert. There I saw a woman sitting on a scarlet beast that was covered with names that insulted God. The beast had seven heads and ten horns.
The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet and was glittering with gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a golden cup full of disgusting things and the filth of her sin.
On her forehead was written a name with a hidden meaning: BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF PROSTITUTES AND OF THE DISGUSTING THINGS OF THE EARTH.
I saw that the woman was drunk with the blood of God's holy people, the blood of those who were killed because they were faithful to Jesus. When I saw her, I was completely amazed.
Then the angel said to me: Why are you amazed? I will explain to you the secret of the woman and of the beast she rides, the one with seven heads and ten horns.

The beast you saw once was, now is not, and will come up out of the deep pit and go to its destruction. The people of the earth whose names have not been written in the book of life since the creation of the world will be amazed when they see the beast, because it once was, now is not, and yet will come.
This calls for a mind with wisdom. The seven heads are seven hills on which the woman sits. They are also seven kings.
Five kings have fallen, one is ruling now, and the other has not yet come. When he does come, he must remain for only a little while.
The beast that once was, and now is not, is itself an eighth king. It belongs with the seven and is going to its destruction.
The ten horns you saw are ten kings who have not yet received a kingdom. But for one hour they will receive authority as kings, along with the beast.
They have one purpose, and they will give their power and authority to the beast.
They will make war against the Lamb, but the Lamb will defeat them, because he is Lord of lords and King of kings. With him will be his called, chosen, and faithful followers.
Then the angel said to me: The waters you saw, where the prostitute sits, are peoples, crowds, nations, and languages.
The beast and the ten horns you saw will hate the prostitute. They will strip her, leave her naked, eat her flesh, and burn her with fire.
For God has put it into their hearts to carry out his purpose — to agree to give their kingdom to the beast until God's words are fulfilled.
The woman you saw is the great city that rules over the kings of the earth.

18.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: After this I saw another angel coming down from heaven. He had great authority, and the earth was made bright by his glory.

He shouted in a powerful voice: Fallen! Fallen is Babylon the Great! She has become a home for demons and a place where every evil spirit lives, every unclean bird, and every hateful and unclean animal.
For all the nations have drunk the wine of her wild and sinful ways. The kings of the earth committed sexual sin with her, and the merchants of the earth grew rich from her great luxury.
Then I heard another voice from heaven say: Come out of her, my people, so that you will not share in her sins and will not receive any of her plagues.
For her sins are piled up as high as heaven, and God has remembered her crimes.
Pay her back the way she paid others. Give her double for what she has done. Mix her a double share in her own cup.
Give her as much torture and sorrow as the glory and luxury she gave herself. In her heart she says: I sit as a queen. I am not a widow, and I will never grieve.
Because of this, her plagues will come in a single day — death, sorrow, and famine. She will be burned up with fire, because the Lord God who judges her is powerful.
The kings of the earth who committed sexual sin with her and shared her luxury will cry and mourn over her when they see the smoke of her burning.
Terrified at her suffering, they will stand far off and say: Disaster! Disaster on you, great city, mighty city of Babylon! In one hour your judgment has come.
The merchants of the earth will cry and mourn over her, because no one buys their goods anymore —
cargoes of gold, silver, jewels, and pearls; fine linen, purple, silk, and scarlet cloth; all kinds of sweet-smelling wood; every kind of object made of ivory, costly wood, bronze, iron, and marble;
cinnamon, spice, incense, myrrh, frankincense, wine, olive oil, fine flour, and wheat; cattle, sheep, horses, and carriages; and slaves — yes, human lives.
They will say: The fruit you longed for is gone from you. All your riches and splendor have vanished, never to be found again.
The merchants who sold these things and grew rich from her will stand far off, terrified at her suffering. They will cry and mourn
and say: Disaster! Disaster on you, great city, dressed in fine linen, purple, and scarlet, and glittering with gold, jewels, and pearls!
In one hour all this great wealth has been destroyed! Every ship captain, all who travel by sea, the sailors, and all who earn their living from the sea will stand far off.
When they see the smoke of her burning, they will cry out: Was there ever a city like this great city?

They will throw dust on their heads, and with crying and mourning they will shout: Disaster! Disaster on you, great city, where all who had ships on the sea grew rich from her wealth! In one hour she has been destroyed.
Be glad over her, you heavens! Be glad, you holy people, apostles, and prophets! For God has judged her for the way she treated you.
Then a mighty angel picked up a stone the size of a large millstone and threw it into the sea. He said: With such force the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, never to be found again.

The music of harpists and musicians, flute players and trumpeters will never be heard in you again. No worker of any trade will ever be found in you again. The sound of a millstone will never be heard in you again.

The light of a lamp will never shine in you again. The voices of the groom and the bride will never be heard in you again. Your merchants were the important people of the world, and all the nations were fooled by your witchcraft.
In her was found the blood of the prophets and of God's holy people, and of all who have been killed on the earth.

19.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: After this I heard what sounded like the roar of a huge crowd in heaven, shouting: Hallelujah! Salvation, glory, and power belong to our God,
because his judgments are true and right. He has judged the great prostitute who ruined the earth with her sin. He has paid her back for the blood of his servants.
Again they shouted: Hallelujah! The smoke from her goes up forever and ever.
The twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshiped God, who was sitting on the throne. They said: Amen! Hallelujah!
Then a voice came from the throne, saying: Praise our God, all you his servants, all you who honor him, both small and great!
Then I heard what sounded like a huge crowd, like the roar of rushing waters and loud peals of thunder, shouting: Hallelujah! For our Lord God Almighty reigns.
Let us be glad and full of joy and give him glory! For the wedding of the Lamb has come, and his bride has made herself ready.
She was given fine linen to wear, bright and clean. (The fine linen stands for the right things done by God's holy people.)
Then the angel said to me: Write this: Blessed are those who are invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb! And he added: These are the true words of God.

I fell down at his feet to worship him. But he said to me: Do not do that! I am a fellow servant with you and with your brothers and sisters who hold to the truth about Jesus. Worship God! For the truth about Jesus is the spirit of prophecy.
I saw heaven standing open, and there before me was a white horse. Its rider is called Faithful and True. He judges and makes war in a way that is right.
His eyes are like flames of fire, and on his head are many crowns. He has a name written on him that no one knows but he himself.
He is dressed in a robe dipped in blood, and his name is the Word of God.
The armies of heaven were following him, riding on white horses and dressed in fine linen, white and clean.
Out of his mouth comes a sharp sword to strike down the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod. He treads the winepress of the furious anger of God Almighty.
On his robe and on his thigh he has this name written: KING OF KINGS AND LORD OF LORDS.
Then I saw an angel standing in the sun. He cried out in a loud voice to all the birds flying high in the sky: Come, gather together for the great supper of God,
so that you may eat the flesh of kings, generals, and mighty men, the flesh of horses and their riders, and the flesh of all people, free and slave, small and great.
Then I saw the beast and the kings of the earth and their armies gathered together to make war against the rider on the horse and his army.

But the beast was captured, and with it the false prophet who had done the miracles for it. With these miracles he had fooled those who had received the mark of the beast and worshiped its image. The two of them were thrown alive into the lake of fire that burns with sulfur.
The rest were killed with the sword that came out of the mouth of the rider on the horse, and all the birds gorged themselves on their flesh.
I, John, saw an angel coming down out of heaven. The angel had the key to the bottomless pit. The angel also held a large chain in his hand.
The angel grabbed the dragon. The dragon is the old snake, the devil, Satan. The angel tied the dragon with the chain for 1000 years.

The angel threw the dragon into the bottomless pit and closed it and locked it. The angel did this so that the dragon could not deceive the people of the nations for 1000 years. But after 1000 years, the dragon must become free and come out for a short time.

Then I, John, saw thrones and people sitting on the thrones. God said those people could judge. The people sitting on the thrones were the believers who had died. People had cut off their heads because they told other people about Jesus and the Word of God. They did not worship the beast and his idol and they did not have the mark of the beast on their foreheads or on their hands. God made them live and they ruled with Christ for 1000 years.
This is the first raising of dead people. God did not make the other dead people live again. He will make them live again after 1 000 years.
Great blessings belong to those who share in this first resurrection. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and for Christ. They will rule with him for 1000 years.
When the 1 000 years are over, God will let Satan out of the jail where he was kept.

He will go out to trick the nations in all the earth, the nations known as Gog and Magog. Satan will gather the people for battle. There will be more people than anyone can count, like sand on the seashore.
I, John, saw Satan’s army march across the earth and gather around the camp of God’s people and the city that God loves. But fire came down from heaven and destroyed Satan’s army.
And the Devil, the one who tricked these people, was thrown into the lake of burning sulfur with the beast and the false prophet. There they would be tortured day and night forever and ever.
Then I saw a big white throne and I saw God sitting on the throne. Earth and sky ran away from him and disappeared

And I saw those who had died, All of them, old people and children, there were standing before God. The books in heaven were opened. And another book was opened, the book of life. God read the things that each person had done. Then God judged them.
The people who died in the sea and the people who died and went to hell stood before God. God then looked in the book and read what they had done, and God judged them.
And Death and Hell were thrown into the lake of fire. This lake of fire is the second death.
If any person’s name was not written in the book of life, then God threw him into the sea of fire.

21.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth. The first heaven and the first earth were gone, and there was no longer any sea.
I saw the holy city, the new Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God. It was beautiful, like a bride dressed for her husband.
I heard a loud voice from the throne say: Look! God's home is now with people. He will live with them, and they will be his people. God himself will be with them and will be their God.
He will wipe away every tear from their eyes. There will be no more death, no more sorrow, no more crying, and no more pain, because the old order of things has passed away.
The One sitting on the throne said: Look, I am making everything new! Then he said: Write this down, because these words are true and trustworthy.
He said to me: It is done. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. To anyone who is thirsty I will give the right to drink freely from the spring of the water of life.
The one who wins the victory will receive all this, and I will be his God, and he will be my child.

But the cowardly, the unbelieving, the disgusting, murderers, those who commit sexual sin, those who practice witchcraft, idol worshipers, and all liars — their place will be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur. This is the second death.
Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues came to me and said: Come, I will show you the bride, the wife of the Lamb.
He carried me away in the Spirit to a great, high mountain and showed me the holy city, Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God.
It shone with the glory of God. Its brightness was like a very precious jewel, like a jasper stone, clear as crystal.
It had a great, high wall with twelve gates, and at the gates were twelve angels. On the gates were written the names of the twelve tribes of Israel.
There were three gates on the east, three on the north, three on the south, and three on the west.
The wall of the city had twelve foundation stones, and on them were the names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
The angel who talked with me had a gold measuring rod to measure the city, its gates, and its wall.
The city was square — its length and width were the same. The angel measured the city with the rod and found it to be about 1,400 miles long, and it was as wide and as high as it was long.
He measured the wall, and it was about 200 feet thick, by human measurement, which the angel was using.
The wall was made of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, as clear as glass.
The foundations of the city wall were decorated with every kind of precious stone. The first foundation was jasper, the second sapphire, the third agate, the fourth emerald,
the fifth onyx, the sixth carnelian, the seventh yellow quartz, the eighth beryl, the ninth topaz, the tenth turquoise, the eleventh jacinth, and the twelfth amethyst.
The twelve gates were twelve pearls, each gate made of a single pearl. The main street of the city was pure gold, as clear as glass.
I did not see a temple in the city, because the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb are its temple.
The city does not need the sun or the moon to shine on it, because the glory of God gives it light, and the Lamb is its lamp.
The nations will walk by its light, and the kings of the earth will bring their glory into it.
Its gates will never be shut, because there will be no night there.
The glory and honor of the nations will be brought into it.
Nothing unclean will ever enter it, nor will anyone who does what is disgusting or false. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will enter.

22.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Then the angel showed me the river of the water of life, clear as crystal, flowing from the throne of God and of the Lamb

down the middle of the city's main street. On each side of the river stood the tree of life, producing twelve kinds of fruit, a fresh crop each month. The leaves of the tree are for the healing of the nations.
There will no longer be any curse. The throne of God and of the Lamb will be in the city, and his servants will serve him.
They will see his face, and his name will be on their foreheads.
There will be no more night. They will not need the light of a lamp or the light of the sun, because the Lord God will give them light. And they will reign forever and ever.
Then the angel said to me: These words are trustworthy and true. The Lord, the God who inspires the prophets, sent his angel to show his servants what must soon happen.
Look, I am coming soon! Blessed is the one who obeys the words of the prophecy in this book.
I, John, am the one who heard and saw these things. And when I had heard and seen them, I fell down to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown them to me.
But he said to me: Do not do that! I am a fellow servant with you, with your brothers and sisters the prophets, and with all who obey the words of this book. Worship God!
Then he told me: Do not keep secret the words of the prophecy in this book, because the time is near.
Let the one who does wrong keep doing wrong, and let the filthy person keep being filthy. Let the one who does right keep doing right, and let the holy person keep being holy.
Look, I am coming soon! I am bringing my reward with me, to give to each person according to what he has done.
I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last, the beginning and the end.
Blessed are those who obey his commands, so that they may have the right to the tree of life and may go through the gates into the city.
Outside are the dogs, those who practice witchcraft, those who commit sexual sin, the murderers, the idol worshipers, and everyone who loves and practices lying.
I, Jesus, have sent my angel to tell you these things for the churches. I am the Root and the Offspring of David, and the bright Morning Star.
The Spirit and the bride say: Come! Let the one who hears say: Come! Let anyone who is thirsty come, and let anyone who wishes take the free gift of the water of life.
I warn everyone who hears the words of the prophecy in this book: If anyone adds anything to them, God will add to that person the plagues described in this book.
And if anyone takes away any words from this book of prophecy, God will take away that person's share in the tree of life and in the holy city, which are described in this book.
The One who tells these things says: Yes, I am coming soon. Amen. Come, Lord Jesus!
May the grace of the Lord Jesus be with all of God's people. Amen.
